acceptance_criteria.criterion: crabInsert's errRestartFromRoot retry loop has a bounded retry count
acceptance_criteria.covered_by: insert.go crabInsert loop now checks attempt >= crabMaxRestarts; TestCrabbingRetryCapSurfacesError (Insert phase)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: crabDeleteOnce's (crabDelete's) errRestartFromRoot retry loop has a bounded retry count
acceptance_criteria.covered_by: delete.go crabDelete loop now checks attempt >= crabMaxRestarts; TestCrabbingRetryCapSurfacesError (Delete phase)

acceptance_criteria.criterion: After the bound is exceeded, surfaces an explicit error rather than retrying forever
acceptance_criteria.covered_by: errTooManyRestarts sentinel returned; test asserts errors.Is(err, errTooManyRestarts) and that the call returns within a bounded wall-clock window instead of hanging

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Documented as a theoretical (never observed) livelock mitigation, not a correctness fix
acceptance_criteria.covered_by: crabMaxRestarts doc comment in insert.go explicitly states this; errRestartFromRoot's existing doc comment (unchanged) already establishes why any retry count is always safe

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Test spec: go test ./engine/btree/... -run TestCrabbingRetryCapSurfacesError -- inject a mechanism forcing TryLock to always fail, assert bounded-retries error not a hang
acceptance_criteria.covered_by: TestCrabbingRetryCapSurfacesError uses permanent real lock contention (precedent: TestCrabbingConcurrentPropagateNoDeadlock) to force every TryLock against the target node to fail for the whole test; asserts errTooManyRestarts returned within a bounded timeout

regression_checks: go test ./engine/... -race (zero regressions vs. baseline; pre-existing engine/split TestReaderDuringSplit flake documented, not chased if isolated)
